primary_concern: Sepsis with multi-organ dysfunction (liver, renal, hematologic)
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection with organ dysfunction. Progressive AKI (Stage 1 to Stage 3) with creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (excluded outlier) indicates acute renal injury. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest coagulopathy and hemolysis, while bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) raises concern for hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA score increase ≥2
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine elevation to 8.9 mg/dL (excluded outlier) meets KDIGO criteria for Stage 3 AKI
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA score criteria for coagulopathy
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection-focused workup
Monitor urine output and renal function hourly
Check INR/PTT and fibrinogen for coagulopathy severity
Consider vasopressor support if hypotension develops
doctor_handoff: Review for septic shock criteria (BP, fluids administered), confirm infection source, and assess for AKI reversal strategies. Evaluate bleeding risk with platelet transfusion threshold discussion.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL
AKI Stage 1 (creatinine 2.1 mg/dL) with platelet decline to 95 K/uL
AKI progression to Stage 3 (creatinine 8.9 mg/dL, excluded outlier)

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
